---
title: "Configuration & Logging"
guide-section: "Operations"
---

# Configuration & Logging

## Environment loading

All environment configuration is read **once, at import time**, in
`constants.py`. It loads `backend/.env` from an absolute path so the result does
not depend on the current working directory, then builds a module-level singleton
(`SINGLETON`). If `.env` is absent, the module logs a warning and falls back to
the ambient environment — the normal case in deployment, where secrets come from
systemd rather than a file.

### Required variables

| Variable                         | Purpose                                            |
| -------------------------------: | :---------------------- |
| `MODEL_NAME`                     | Gemini model identifier                            |
| `GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT`           | GCP project ID                                     |
| `GOOGLE_CLOUD_LOCATION`          | Vertex AI compute region for the LLM               |
| `GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS` | GCP credentials — a file path **or** inline JSON   |
| `VERTEX_AI_DATASTORE_LAWS`       | Datastore ID for the housing-law corpus (required) |

: Required environment variables {#tbl-required-env}

Credentials are resolved by
[`load_gcp_credentials`](../reference/google_auth.load_gcp_credentials.qmd), which
accepts either a path to a credentials file or the JSON content itself (for
environments like LangSmith Cloud, where secrets are injected as env-var values).

### Datastores

Any variable named `VERTEX_AI_DATASTORE_<NAME>` is collected into a
name→id mapping, keyed by `<NAME>` lowercased. `VERTEX_AI_DATASTORE_LAWS` is
required; others (e.g. `VERTEX_AI_DATASTORE_OREGON_LAW_HELP`) enable additional
RAG tools when present. The value may be a bare datastore ID or a full
`projects/.../dataStores/<id>` resource URI — the latter is reduced to its ID.

### Optional and fixed settings

- `SHOW_MODEL_THINKING` (default `false`) — surface model reasoning as
  `ReasoningChunk`s. Intended for staging only.
- Model tuning is currently fixed in code for reproducible legal output:
  temperature `0.1`, top-p `0.1`, max tokens `65535`, and a dynamic thinking
  budget.

::: {.callout-warning}
Safety filters are turned off so statutory discussion (eviction, harassment,
habitability) is not blocked, and `SHOW_MODEL_THINKING` exposes raw model
reasoning. Keep `SHOW_MODEL_THINKING` off in production and treat the disabled
safety filters as a deliberate, staging-aware trade-off.
:::

See [Deployment.md](https://github.com/codeforpdx/tenantfirstaid/blob/main/Deployment.md)
for how these values are managed in production.

## Logging

Logging is centralized in `logger.py`. Entrypoints (`app.py`, the evaluation
runner) call `configure_logging()` once to install a single stderr handler with a
shared format. The level defaults to `DEBUG` when `ENV=dev` and `INFO` otherwise.

The formatter colorizes `WARNING`/`ERROR`/`CRITICAL` level names only when stderr
is a TTY, so log files and CI captures stay free of ANSI escapes.
`configure_logging()` is idempotent — repeated imports under pytest or Gunicorn
workers do not double-install handlers. For the narrow case where `constants.py`
must emit a formatted warning during import (before any entrypoint has run),
`temporary_formatted_handler()` attaches the project formatter to a single logger
for the duration of a `with` block.

## Where to go next

- [Corpus Ingestion](07-corpus-ingestion.qmd) — build the datastore these
  variables point at.
